markdown.markdown:
```{r} #| label: Personal Consumption #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(dplyr) library(plotly) spending <- readRDS("data/fred_spending.rds") fig <- plot_ly(spending, x = ~date) fig <- fig %>% add_lines(y = ~disposable_income, name = "Disposable Income") fig <- fig %>% add_lines(y = ~durable_goods, name = "Durable Goods") fig <- fig %>% add_lines(y = ~nondurable_goods, name = "Nondurable Goods") fig <- fig %>% add_lines(y = ~services, name = "Services") fig <- fig %>% layout( title = list( text = paste( "Real Personal Consumption Expenditures and Disposable Personal Income", "
Seasonally Adjusted" ) ), shapes = list( list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2007-12-01", x1 = "2009-06-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ), list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2020-02-01", x1 = "2020-04-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ) ), xaxis = list( title = "Date" ), yaxis = list( title = "Billions of Chained 2017 Dollars" ), annotations = list( list( text = "Source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), FRED", xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0, y = -0.30, showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list(size = 10) ), list( x = "2008-09-01", y = 0.95, xref = "x", yref = "paper", text = "Great Recession", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 10) ), list( x = "2020-03-01", y = 0.95, xref = "x", yref = "paper", text = "COVID Recession", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 10) ) ), margin = list( t = 80, b = 120 ) ) config( fig, displayModeBar = FALSE ) ``` ```{r} #| label: Vehicle Sales and Fuel Prices #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(plotly) autoFuel <- readRDS("data/vehicleSalesFuelPrices.rds") %>% filter(date >= as.Date("2007-01-01")) %>% mutate( value.y = as.numeric(value.y) ) fig <- plot_ly(autoFuel, x = ~date) fig <- fig %>% add_lines( y = ~value.x, name = "Light Vehicle Sales", yaxis = "y1" ) fig <- fig %>% add_lines( y = ~value.y, name = "Fuel Prices", yaxis = "y2" ) fig %>% layout( title = list( text = "Light Vehicle Sales and Fuel Prices
United States, Monthly Data Since 2007" ), shapes = list( list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2007-12-01", x1 = "2009-06-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ), list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2020-02-01", x1 = "2020-04-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ) ), xaxis = list( title = "Date", showline = TRUE, linewidth = 1 ), yaxis = list( title = "Light Vehicle Sales (Millions)", side = "left", range = c(1,20) ), yaxis2 = list( title = "Fuel Prices ($ per Gallon)", overlaying = "y", side = "right", range = c(1,20) ), legend = list( x = 1.05, y = 1, xanchor = "left" ), annotations = list( list( text = "Source: U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA), Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED)", xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0, y = -0.25, showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list(size = 10) ), list( x = "2008-09-01", y = 0.95, xref = "x", yref = "paper", text = "Great Recession", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 10) ), list( x = "2020-03-01", y = 0.95, xref = "x", yref = "paper", text = "COVID Recession", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 10) ) ), margin = list( t = 60, b = 100, r = 120 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ``` ```{r} #| label: GDP Percent Change #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(plotly) gdp <- readRDS("data/gdpPercentChange.rds") %>% mutate(TimePeriod = as.Date(paste0(TimePeriod, "-01-01"))) mi_gdp <- gdp %>% select(TimePeriod, Michigan_GDP_percent_change) %>% distinct() %>% arrange(TimePeriod) counties <- unique(gdp$GeoName) init <- counties[1] p <- plot_ly() for (cty in counties) { df <- gdp %>% filter(GeoName == cty) p <- p %>% add_lines( data = df, x = ~TimePeriod, y = ~GDP_percent_change, name = cty, visible = (cty == init), line = list(color = "steelblue"), legendgroup = "county" ) } p <- p %>% add_lines( data = mi_gdp, x = ~TimePeriod, y = ~Michigan_GDP_percent_change, name = "Michigan", line = list(color = "black", dash = "dash"), inherit = FALSE ) buttons <- lapply(seq_along(counties), function(i) { vis <- rep(FALSE, length(counties) + 1) vis[i] <- TRUE vis[length(vis)] <- TRUE # Michigan always visible list( method = "update", args = list( list(visible = vis), list(title = paste("GDP Growth Rate:", counties[i], "vs Michigan")) ), label = counties[i] ) }) p %>% layout( title = paste("GDP Growth Rate:", init, "vs Michigan"), xaxis = list( title = "Year", type = "date", tickformat = "%Y", dtick = "M12" ), yaxis = list( title = "GDP Percent Change" ), updatemenus = list( list( buttons = buttons, x = 1.4, y = 0.75, xanchor = "right" ) ), annotations = list( list( text = "Source: US BEA (table CAGDP1), Upjohn Regional.", xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0, y = -0.25, showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list(size = 10) )), legend = list( x = 1.05, y = 1, xanchor = "left" ), margin = list( r = 200, t = 30, b = 100 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ``` ```{r} #| label: Business Applications Change From a Year Ago #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(plotly) library(dplyr) biz <- readRDS("data/businessAppChange.rds") %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(date)) %>% arrange(Date) fig <- plot_ly(biz, x = ~Date) fig <- fig %>% add_lines( y = ~Michigan, name = "Michigan" ) fig <- fig %>% add_lines( y = ~United_States, name = "United States", line = list(dash = "dash") ) fig %>% layout( title = list( text = "Business Applications Growth Rate
Michigan vs United States" ), xaxis = list( title = "", showline = TRUE ), yaxis = list( title = "Percent Change", ticksuffix = "%", zeroline = TRUE, zerolinecolor = "gray" ), legend = list( x = 1.05, y = 1, xanchor = "left" ), shapes = list( # Great Recession list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2007-12-01", x1 = "2009-06-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ), # COVID recession list( type = "rect", xref = "x", yref = "paper", x0 = "2020-02-01", x1 = "2020-04-01", y0 = 0, y1 = 1, fillcolor = "lightgray", opacity = 0.3, line = list(width = 0), layer = "below" ) ), annotations = list( list( text = "Source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (series BUSAPPWNSAMIYY and BUSAPPWNSAUS), Upjohn Institute, Updated February 2026.", xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0, y = -0.25, showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list(size = 10) ) ), margin = list( t = 60, b = 100 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ``` ```{r} #| label: Average Annual Wage #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(plotly) library(dplyr) pay <- readRDS("data/averageAnnualWage.rds") %>% mutate( year_MI = as.Date(paste0(year_MI, "-01-01")), value_MI = as.numeric(value_MI), value_US = as.numeric(value_US), industry = sub("^NAICS\\s+", "", seriesID) ) %>% arrange(industry, year_MI) # Industries in the order they appear in the data industries <- unique(pay$industry) # Initial industry init <- industries[1] df_init <- pay %>% filter(industry == init) # Create plot fig <- plot_ly() # Michigan trace fig <- fig %>% add_lines( data = df_init, x = ~year_MI, y = ~value_MI, name = "Michigan", line = list(color = "steelblue") ) # United States trace fig <- fig %>% add_lines( data = df_init, x = ~year_MI, y = ~value_US, name = "United States", line = list(color = "black", dash = "dash") ) # Dropdown buttons buttons <- lapply(seq_along(industries), function(i){ df <- pay %>% filter(industry == industries[i]) list( method = "restyle", args = list( list( x = list( df$year_MI, df$year_MI ), y = list( df$value_MI, df$value_US ) ), c(0,1) ), label = industries[i] ) }) # Layout fig %>% layout( title = list( text = paste( "Average Annual Pay:", init ), x = 0.5 ), xaxis = list( title = "", type = "date", tickformat = "%Y" ), yaxis = list( title = "Average Annual Pay", tickprefix = "$", tickformat = ",d", separatethousands = TRUE ), updatemenus = list( list( type = "dropdown", direction = "down", active = 0, buttons = buttons, x = 0.5, y = 1.18, xanchor = "center", yanchor = "top" ) ), legend = list( orientation = "h", x = 0.35, y = 1.08 ), annotations = list( list( text = "Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages (QCEW) • Wages represent total private ownership", xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0, y = -0.25, showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list(size = 10) ) ), margin = list( t = 110, r = 40, b = 100 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ``` ```{r} #| label: Class of Worker #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false library(dplyr) library(plotly) library(tidyr) # Read data workers <- readRDS("data/classOfWorker.rds") # Convert to long format workers_long <- workers %>% pivot_longer( cols = c( "Private Wage and Salary Workers", "Government Workers", "Self-employed in own, not incorporated, business workers", "Unpaid family workers" ), names_to = "WorkerClass", values_to = "Percent" ) # Worker colors worker_colors <- c( "Private Wage and Salary Workers" = "#01665e", "Government Workers" = "#5ab4ac", "Self-employed in own, not incorporated, business workers" = "#d8b365", "unpaid family workers" = "#8c510a" ) # Counties all_counties <- unique(workers_long$NAME) init <- all_counties[1] # Create legend annotations legend_annotations <- list( list( x = 1.02, y = 1.00, xref = "paper", yref = "paper", text = "Class of Worker", showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left" ) ) # Add worker categories to legend annotations y_pos <- c(.92, .84, .76, .68) for(i in seq_along(worker_colors)) { legend_annotations <- append( legend_annotations, list( list( x = 1.02, y = y_pos[i], xref = "paper", yref = "paper", text = paste0( "■ ", names(worker_colors)[i] ), font = list( color = worker_colors[i] ), showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left" ) ) ) } # Create traces p <- plot_ly() for (county in all_counties) { df_county <- workers_long %>% filter(NAME == county) for (worker in names(worker_colors)) { value <- df_county %>% filter(WorkerClass == worker) %>% pull(Percent) p <- p %>% add_trace( x = county, y = value, type = "bar", name = worker, legendgroup = worker, marker = list( color = worker_colors[worker] ), visible = (county == all_counties[1]), showlegend = FALSE ) } } # Dropdown buttons buttons <- lapply(seq_along(all_counties), function(i){ vis <- rep(FALSE, length(all_counties) * length(worker_colors)) start <- (i - 1) * length(worker_colors) + 1 end <- start + length(worker_colors) - 1 vis[start:end] <- TRUE list( method = "update", args = list( list( visible = vis ), list( title = paste( "Class of Worker -", all_counties[i] ) ) ), label = all_counties[i] ) }) # Plot p %>% layout( title = paste( "Class of Worker -", all_counties[1] ), barmode = "stack", xaxis = list( title = "" ), yaxis = list( title = "Percent", range = c(0,100) ), updatemenus = list( list( buttons = buttons, active = 0, x = 1.05, xanchor = "left", y = 0.5, yanchor = "top" ) ), showlegend = FALSE, annotations = c( legend_annotations, list( list( x = 0, y = -0.15, xref = "paper", yref = "paper", text = "Source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2024 ACS 5-Year Estimates", showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list( size = 11, color = "gray" ) ) ) ), margin = list( r = 300, b = 100 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ```

Plot p %>% layout( title = paste( "Class of Worker -", all_counties[1] ), barmode = "stack", xaxis = list( title = "" ), yaxis = list( title = "Percent", range = c(0,100) ), updatemenus = list( list( buttons = buttons, active = 0, x = 1.05, xanchor = "left", y = 0.5, yanchor = "top" ) ), showlegend = FALSE, annotations = c( legend_annotations, list( list( x = 0, y = -0.15, xref = "paper", yref = "paper", text = "Source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2024 ACS 5-Year Estimates", showarrow = FALSE, xanchor = "left", font = list( size = 11, color = "gray" ) ) ) ), margin = list( r = 300, b = 100 ) ) %>% config(displayModeBar = FALSE) ```

language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: For attribution, please cite this work as:
